title: potentilla glandulosa f. ashlandica
intro: Potentilla glandulosa f. ashlandica, also known as Ashland cinquefoil, is a subspecies of the Potentilla glandulosa plant, a flowering perennial native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to its high content of tannins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Ashland cinquefoil include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Ashland cinquefoil can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for medicinal purposes, and it has also been used in traditional folk medicine as a treatment for various skin conditions and infections.
